content: Drawing on Merriam-Webster’s definition of an agent as any entity that exerts power to produce an effect, we can frame the 2024-2025 cutting-edge active learning AI agent memory systems as functional 'biological agents' for long-horizon AI adaptation, where memory retention acts as the active pathogenic mechanism that drives persistent, context-aware change in the agent’s behavior over time. Just as biological agents use stored genetic and adaptive information to alter host systems, modern AI memory agents use their structured memory stores to alter the LLM’s default static outputs to fit evolving long-horizon goals.
